newsSinceJun6: No material fundamental news since June 6. June 9's -5.6% pullback looks flow-driven: profit-taking near 52-week highs after a +113% YTD run, amplified by June 8 retail coverage of director 10b5-1 sales (Raza ~$779k on June 3, Viana ~$761k on June 4 - pre-planned, not signal). Last real catalysts (record Q1 ACV+royalties $92.8M +39% y/y; MIPS FlexGen collaboration) all pre-date June 6.

sources.label: Motley Fool - Arteris director sold over 20,000 shares (June 8, 2026)
Investing.com - Arteris director Antonio Viana sells $760,879 in stock
Investing.com - Arteris director Atiq Raza sells $779k in stock

verdicts.rankRationale: Hold at 16: no new fundamental evidence since yesterday's hold, and the -5.6% flow-driven dip (profit-taking plus 10b5-1 insider-sale headlines) with the on-chip interconnect/NoC complexity-scaling thesis fully intact marginally improves entry asymmetry rather than degrading it. Not sharp enough, and the name is too discovered post-rerating (+113% YTD, ~380% 1-yr), to justify moving up.
verdicts.bull: NoC interconnect IP is a per-chip complexity toll on every large AI SoC; record Q1 ACV+royalties of $92.8M (+39% y/y) and the MIPS FlexGen tie-up show design-win momentum, and a 5.6% pullback on zero news is a cheaper entry into an intact thesis.
verdicts.neutral: At ~$1.5B cap after a +113% YTD rerating the hidden-bottleneck discount has largely been paid; royalty ramp must now outrun an already-rerated multiple, so drift until the next design-win or earnings datapoint.
verdicts.bear: Post-380% 1-yr run the name is crowded for its size; clustered director selling near the highs and a richly valued, still small-revenue base ($22.9M/quarter) leave plenty of air below if AI SoC license activity decelerates.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Q2 earnings / next ACV-plus-royalties print and incremental AI-silicon design wins (FlexGen adoption following the MIPS collaboration).
verdicts.primaryRisk: Multiple compression in a crowded small-cap AI trade; license/royalty growth deceleration would hit a stock priced for continued 35-40% growth.
